settings.text: If the business is growing, the brand should be able to keep up.
Pixelcraft is best for founders and small teams who need a tighter visual system, sharper launch assets, or clearer rollout design before the next product, opening, or campaign.

settings.title: Practical info before you reach out
settings.description: A few notes on fit, availability, and how projects usually begin.

settings.title: Pixelcraft
settings.text: Independent graphic design studio focused on identity systems, campaign design, and launch-ready assets for small businesses.

settings.title: Best Fit
settings.text:
Founder-led brands
Product launches
Hospitality and retail businesses
Cultural campaigns

settings.title: Availability
settings.text:
Booking 3-6 weeks ahead for full systems.
Smaller launch support can sometimes begin sooner.

settings.title: Common questions before kickoff
settings.description: A quick way to understand timelines, scope, and how collaboration usually works.

settings.question: What kinds of projects are the best fit?
settings.answer: The best projects have a clear business need behind them: a launch, a repositioning, a new product line, or a brand that has outgrown improvised visuals.

settings.question: Do you only design logos?
settings.answer: No. Pixelcraft works on systems. A logo can be part of the work, but the goal is usually a stronger visual framework plus the assets needed to use it well.

settings.question: How long does a project usually take?
settings.answer: Focused launch support can move quickly. Full identity systems usually take several weeks, depending on how many rollout assets and review rounds are involved.

settings.question: Can you work with an existing website or team?
settings.answer: Yes. Many projects involve clarifying the visual system first, then handing clean direction and assets to an internal team or web partner.

settings.title: Before you write
settings.text:
Typical projects start around $2,500 for focused launch support and scale upward for full identity systems.

It helps to share your timeline, business type, and what currently feels unclear or inconsistent.
